skill.name: aws-skills
skill.description: AWS development with infrastructure automation and cloud architecture patterns
skill.summary: AWS development with infrastructure automation and cloud architecture patterns
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two GitHub URLs and two weak-cryptography patterns. Review found the URLs are source reference links only, and the weak-cryptography hits are prose or metadata, not executable cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Source Repository Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22. These are GitHub source reference links in metadata and documentation, with no code that performs network access or data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain static GitHub links only. No executable request, fetch, curl, or network-capable script is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20. The cited lines contain descriptive text about AWS development and architecture patterns, not cryptographic functions or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file has no executable code and no cryptographic implementation. The finding appears caused by matching text inside ordinary prose or metadata.

content.value_statement: AWS projects often need consistent guidance for infrastructure, automation, and architecture decisions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise context for AWS development planning.
actual_capabilities: Guides AWS development discussions using infrastructure automation context.
Frames cloud architecture tasks around practical AWS patterns.
Helps plan when to use AWS-focused development assistance.
Provides a compact skill description for AI coding tools.
Links users to the upstream source repository for more context.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, templates, or deployment commands.
Does not validate live AWS account configuration.
Does not replace AWS security review or cost governance.
Provides high-level guidance rather than service-specific implementation steps.

use_cases.title: Plan Infrastructure Automation
use_cases.description: Use the skill to structure AWS automation work before writing deployment assets.

use_cases.title: Review Cloud Architecture Direction
use_cases.description: Use the skill to keep architecture discussions focused on AWS development patterns.

use_cases.title: Prepare AWS Development Tasks
use_cases.description: Use the skill to brief an AI coding assistant before implementing AWS-related changes.

prompt_templates.title: Describe an AWS Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the aws-skills context to summarize this AWS development task and list the main infrastructure concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Automation Steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the aws-skills context to outline an AWS infrastructure automation plan for this project requirement.

prompt_templates.title: Review Architecture Choices
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the aws-skills context to review these AWS architecture choices for reliability, operations, and maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Implementation Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the aws-skills context to create implementation guidance for an AWS development change, including assumptions, risks, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Plan an AWS automation task for a new service.
output_examples.output: A concise plan covering target services, infrastructure boundaries, deployment flow, operational checks, and review points.
A structured review that highlights expected benefits, tradeoffs, reliability concerns, and follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: Prepare guidance for an AWS development change.
output_examples.output: A practical task brief with assumptions, implementation stages, test expectations, and deployment considerations.

best_practices: Provide the target AWS services, account boundaries, and deployment environment.
Ask for security, reliability, and cost considerations during planning.
Validate generated guidance against current AWS documentation and internal policies.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as the only source for production security decisions.
Do not ask it to infer account details that were not provided.
Do not deploy infrastructure changes without independent review and testing.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants frame AWS development, infrastructure automation, and cloud architecture tasks.

faq.question: Does it run AWS commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains guidance text and links only.

faq.question: Can it create deployment templates?
faq.answer: It can help plan template requirements, but this package does not include ready-made templates.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The flagged patterns were reviewed as false positives, and no executable risky behavior was found.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Should users verify AWS guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. Users should check current AWS documentation and their organization policies before production use.
